name: Ph.D. in Transportation Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The eleven referenced courses include a clear research spine (TRAN 725/726 Independent Study, TRAN 791 Doctoral Seminar at 0 credits, TRAN 792B Pre-Doctoral Research) plus infrastructure-oriented electives (CE 613 Resilient Systems Planning and Design, CE 671 Performance and Risk Analysis of Infrastructure Systems), but the substantive electives shown skew heavily to water and environmental topics (CE 620 Open Channel Flow, CE 621 Hydrology, ENE 672 Stormwater Management, ENE 673 Sustainability and Life Cycle Analysis) rather than core transportation subjects, so the transportation identity of the doctorate is weakly represented on the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the transportation-core graduate courses (traffic flow theory, travel-demand/network modeling, ITS, pavement/materials, transportation safety) so the doctorate reads as transportation rather than water-resources engineering.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 at the graduate level; the visible prerequisites (e.g., CE 613 lists CS 101, MATH 211, MATH 279, CE 320, and CE 620/621 list undergraduate fluid mechanics) reach back to undergraduate courses rather than forming a graduate chain, which is reasonable for a doctorate. The single missingCourseCount is TRAN 790A, the dissertation course, so the one dangling reference is the culminating vehicle.
dimensions.suggestion: Add TRAN 790A to the catalog and introduce an explicit graduate research-methods prerequisite ahead of the pre-doctoral research so a minimal doctoral sequence is visible.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, so applicants cannot judge the transportation-research competencies (modeling, data analysis, dissertation-quality investigation) the doctorate certifies.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 4-5 Ph.D. learning outcomes describing transportation-specific research, modeling, and dissemination competencies.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 12 with creditReconcileDelta null; the 12 credits reflect the research/seminar block, not the full doctoral total (typically 60+ post-B.S. or ~30+ post-M.S.), so the coursework-versus-research split is not transparent.
dimensions.suggestion: State the full doctoral credit requirement and how required coursework, dissertation research (TRAN 792B/790A), and seminar divide it.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Presentation is uneven: several electives have clear descriptions (CE 613, CE 620, CE 621, CE 671, ENE 672, ENE 673), but TRAN 725 and TRAN 726 have empty descriptions and the TRAN 792B Pre-Doctoral Research entry carries clearly mismatched text beginning 'Biomedical Engineering Biomedical engineering is currently the fastest growing field of engineering', a data error that undermines the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Correct the garbled TRAN 792B description and fill the empty TRAN 725/726 descriptions so the research vehicles are accurately represented.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The infrastructure-resilience electives shown (CE 613 Resilient Systems, CE 671 Infrastructure Risk/Asset Management, ENE 673 Sustainability and Life Cycle Analysis) are current and relevant to modern resilient-infrastructure practice, but core transportation currency (intelligent transportation systems, connected/automated vehicles, transportation data analytics, travel-demand modeling, safety) is absent from the visible courses.
dimensions.suggestion: Add and surface modern transportation electives (ITS/CAV, transportation data analytics, network modeling, safety analytics) so the doctorate's currency in its named field is demonstrable.

descriptionQuality.justification: Six of the substantive electives (CE 613, CE 620, CE 621, CE 671, ENE 672, ENE 673) carry clear, specific descriptions, but TRAN 725/726 are empty and TRAN 792B contains mismatched Biomedical-Engineering text, so description quality is adequate overall but marred by real data errors.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites, where present, are stated clearly (CE 613's explicit list; CE 620/621's undergraduate fluid mechanics) and are internally consistent; the TRAN research vehicles list none, which is expected, and the only dangling reference is TRAN 790A.

missingTopics.rationale: No core traffic-flow, capacity, or operations course is visible, a fundamental gap for a doctorate named in Transportation Engineering.
Four-step/activity-based demand modeling and network equilibrium are staple transportation-research tools absent from the referenced courses.

missingTopics.topic: Intelligent transportation systems and connected/automated vehicles
missingTopics.rationale: ITS and CAV research define the field's frontier but appear nowhere among the visible electives.
Crash modeling and large-scale mobility-data analytics are central to modern transportation doctorates yet are not surfaced.

emergingTrends.rationale: CAV control, cooperative driving, and infrastructure readiness are the leading transportation-research direction the doctorate should foreground.
Machine learning on GPS/probe and sensor data is transforming transportation research and pairs with the program's infrastructure-risk courses.
EV charging, energy, and decarbonization of transport are fast-growing research areas complementing ENE 673's life-cycle focus.
CE 613 and CE 671 signal a resilience thread the program can formalize into a coherent resilient-mobility research agenda.

topSuggestions: Correct the garbled TRAN 792B description (mismatched Biomedical-Engineering text) and populate the empty TRAN 725/726 descriptions.
Surface transportation-core graduate courses (traffic flow, travel-demand/network modeling, ITS/CAV, safety) so the doctorate reflects its named field rather than water resources.
Resolve the dangling TRAN 790A dissertation reference and add an explicit research-methods prerequisite.
State the full doctoral credit total and its coursework-versus-research split so the 12-credit figure is not read as the whole degree.
